title: Disclosed conflict: the US government's three hats on AI
intro: A government source commenting on AI holds three positions at once: it regulates the technology, buys it, and holds equity in the stack it comments on. A government AI claim therefore moves three books: policy, procurement and portfolio. Agency primary data (Federal Reserve, BLS, USGS) stays tier 1; this flag applies to government commentary and claims on AI.

hats.detail: Gates model releases and sets AI policy (2026 safety-review holds on GPT-5.6 and Fable 5 / Mythos 5).
Procures AI and awards or withholds federal contracts (Anthropic reportedly frozen out of the stake talks and blocked from federal contracts, mid-2026).
Holds equity across the AI-buildout stack, and was offered a 5% stake in a model lab (OpenAI).

roster_note: CFR tracker: roughly $20.9B across 17 equity or quasi-equity deals since Jan 2025 (a broader count reaches 28 firms once letters of intent are included). By dollars: semiconductors ~52%, critical minerals ~43%. Held equity below; revenue-share and proposed positions are kept separate so they are not miscounted.

roster.position: 5% (some sources 10%) + 5% of the GM Thacker Pass JV
golden share (veto + board seat, no economic stake)

separate: Nvidia: a 25% cut of its China chip sales. Revenue-share, not ownership.
OpenAI: 5% offered (~$42.6B on the $852B March-2026 valuation); an early-stage proposal, not held, reportedly with no board seat or vote.
Trump personal / brokerage buys (Broadcom, Synopsys, Dell, Intel in Q1) are a separate entity from the federal stakes and are not folded into this roster.
sources: CNBC, CFR government-deals tracker (via NAI500), Motley Fool, Built In, 24/7 Wall St, TheStreet, CNN, Time, TechCrunch, NOTUS, Crypto Briefing. Verified 2026-07-13.
coi: This board's author is assisted by an Anthropic model, and Anthropic is named in this reporting (reportedly frozen out of the stake talks and blocked from federal contracts). Non-neutral on this node; carried as reported.
